title: Canadian Survey of Consumer Expectations—Survey Data - Bank of Canada
content:
## Canadian Survey of Consumer Expectations—Survey Data
View data from the Canadian Survey of Consumer Expectations (CSCE)—a quarterly survey aimed at measuring household views of inflation, the labour market and household finances.

## Inflation and interest rates

### Chart 1: Inflation expectations and perceptions

- Save image ... PNG JPG PDF
- Download all ... CSV JSON XML
- Download date range ... CSV JSON XML
- 1px 2px 4px 10px 200 300 400 800 10 15 20 30 20% 40% 60% 80% 100%
- 200 300 400 800 10 15 20 30
- 20% 40% 60% 80% 100%
Inflation expectations for each horizon: What do you expect the rate of inflation (deflation) to be? Perceptions about current inflation: What do you think the rate of inflation (deflation) was over the last 12 months?
1-year-ahead inflation expectations
2-year-ahead inflation expectations
5-year-ahead inflation expectations
Perceptions about current inflation

### Chart 2: Inflation expectations: uncertainty

- 1px 2px 4px 10px 200 300 400 800 10 15 20 30 20% 40% 60% 80% 100%
- 200 300 400 800 10 15 20 30

In your view, what would you say is the percent chance that, over the next 12 months (in 12 to 24 months / five years), the rate of inflation or deflation will be between 0% and 2%, 2% and 4% ...? (For example, there is ___ percent chance that the rate of inflation or deflation will be between 0% and 2%, 2% and 4% ...)
Uncertainty, 1-year-ahead inflation expectations
Uncertainty, 2-year-ahead inflation expectations
Uncertainty, 5-year-ahead inflation expectations

## Household finance, spending and sentiment

### Chart 5: Financial well-being
- Save image ... PNG JPG PDF Download all ... CSV JSON XML Download date range ... CSV JSON XML Print Annotate ...
- Save image ... PNG JPG PDF
- Download all ... CSV JSON XML
- Download date range ... CSV JSON XML
- 1px 2px 4px 10px 200 300 400 800 10 15 20 30 20% 40% 60% 80% 100%
- 200 300 400 800 10 15 20 30
- 20% 40% 60% 80% 100%
Previous 12 months: Do you think you (and any family living with you) are financially better or worse off these days than you were 12 months ago? Next 12 months: And looking ahead, do you think you (and any family living with you) will be financially better or worse off 12 months from now than you are these days?
Financial well-being compared to 12 months ago
Financial well-being 12 months from now

### Chart 6: Access to credit

Previous 12 months: Compared to 12 months ago, do you think it is generally harder or easier these days for people to obtain credit or loans (including credit and retail cards, auto loans, student loans, and mortgages)?  Next 12 months: And looking ahead, do you think that 12 months from now it will generally be harder or easier for people to obtain credit or loans (including credit and retail cards, auto loans, student loans, and mortgages) than it is these days?
Access to credit compared to 12 months ago
Access to credit 12 months from now

Probability of missing a debt payment

Push for increased pay with current employer
Bring forward major purchases
Cut spending or push back major purchases
Shop around for better deals

Expected spending growth, next 12 months

### Chart 12: Moving, selling or buying intentions

- Download date range ... CSV JSON XML
- 1px 2px 4px 10px 200 300 400 800 10 15 20 30 20% 40% 60% 80% 100%

- 20% 40% 60% 80% 100%
Probability of moving: For example, what do you think is the percent chance that over the next 12 months you will move to a different primary residence (that is, the place where you usually live)? Probability of selling: What do you think is the percent chance that, over the next 12 months, you will put your primary residence on the market for sale? Buying intentions: Are you planning or thinking of buying a house or a condo now  or have you bought one during the last year?
More than 50% chance of moving in next 12 months
More than 50% chance of selling in next 12 months
Planning to buy or thinking of buying a house or condo
Renters planning to buy in next 12 months

- Save image ... PNG JPG PDF

What do you think is the most likely outlook for the Canadian economy over the next 12 months?
Stable or little change
Some economic growth
Strong economic growth

### Chart 15: Labour turnover
Probability of losing job: What do you think is the percent chance that you will lose your job during the next 12 months? Probability of leaving job voluntarily: What do you think is the percent chance that you will leave your job voluntarily during the next 12 months? Probability of finding job: Suppose you were to lose your job. What do you think is the percent chance that within the following 3 months you would find a job that you would accept?
Probability of losing job in the next 12 months (left scale)
Probability of leaving job voluntarily in the next 12 months (left scale)
Probability of finding job in the next 3 months if lost main job this month (right scale)

### Chart 16: Wage growth expectations
Wage growth expectations: By about what percent do you expect your earnings* to have increased (decreased) over the next 12 months? Wage growth perceptions: By about what percent do you think your earnings have increased (decreased) over the past 12 months?
1-year-ahead inflation expectations
Expected wage growth, next 12 months
Perceptions about wage growth, past 12 months
